title: Tetramolopium macrum
taxonomy: Tetramolopium macrum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which includes daisies and sunflowers. This plant is part of the genus Tetramolopium, which consists of a group of Australian shrubs. Tetramolopium macrum is native to Australia and is often found growing in coastal areas. intro: Tetramolopium macrum, also known as Queensland copperleaf, is a flowering plant species in the family Acanthaceae, native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in treating various health conditions such as fever, sore throats, and skin infections.
